verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (cysteine-rich secretory protein 1) is a GPI-anchored protein expressed on the surface of immune cells, particularly T cells and NK cells. It is anchored to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane via a GPI anchor, making it stably accessible from the extracellular space. The protein has a well-characterized role in immune regulation and cell-cell interactions. GPI anchoring is a canonical outer-leaflet anchoring mechanism, and CRISP1 is robustly detected on intact cells by flow cytometry and surface proteomics, confirming stable outer-leaflet residence.
